title: Single Nucleus RNA-Seq using snPATHO-Seq
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7kc1v","text":"This protocol details the single nucleus RNA-Seq using snPATHO-Seq.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Single Nucleus RNA sequencing from FFPE blocks followed by the snPATHO-Seq protocol ( https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.12.07.570700).

Briefly, two 30-50 µm thick scrolls from each sample, adjacent to sections for CosMx, Xenium, and H&E staining, were cut and placed them into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes, stored them at 4 °C inside Ziplock bags with desiccants to control moisture. Scrolls were stored for up to 1 week.

For the preparation of nuclei suspensions, the scrolls sections were subjected to triple wash in 1 mL of xylene (214736, Millipore Sigma) for 10 minutes each to dewax, followed by rehydration through sequential ethanol (E7023, Millipore Sigma) immersions of decreasing concentrations (100% twice, then 70%, 50%, and finally 30%), each for a duration of 00:01:00 .
Of note, the initial xylene wash was performed at 55 °C for 00:10:00 to enhance paraffin removal.
Subsequent to rehydration, a single wash was performed using 1 mL of wash RPMI 1640 media (11875093, Gibco).

After removing the RPMI1640 wash, add 50 µL - 100 µL of digestion mix [RPMI1640 supplemented with 1 mg/mL of Liberase TH (5401151001, Millipore-Sigma), along with 1 U/µL RNAse Inhibitor (RiboLock RNase Inhibitor, EO0382, Thermo Fisher Scientific)] was added to the tissue scrolls breaking them to smaller pieces with a pestle before topping up to 1 mL with the same digestion media.
Digestion was carried out in a Thermomixer at 800 rpm, , .
After digestion, 400 µL of Ez Lysis Buffer (NUC-101, Sigma-Aldrich) was added, mixed by inversion five times, and centrifuged at 850 rcf, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
The sample was then filtered through a 70 µm strainer (pluriStrainer Mini 70µm, 43-10070-50, pluriSelect) and centrifuged, with the flow-through washed again with 800 µL of the lysis mix.
Nuclei pellet was washed three times in 1x PBS (70011044, Gibco) with 1% BSA, followed by two washes in 0.5x PBS (70011044, Gibco) with 0.02% BSA.
Nuclei was finally resuspended in a volume of 500 µL - 1000 µL of the same buffer, dependent on pellet size, and filtered through a 40 µm strainer (pluriStrainer Mini 40μm, 43-10040-50, pluriSelect).
Nuclei were counted using a Luna-FX7 using Acridine Orange/Propidium Iodide Stain (Logos).

For immediate analysis, the suspension was kept on wet ice until processing using the Chromium X controller and the Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling Reagent Kits for multiplexed samples (Chromium Fixed RNA Kit, Human Transcriptome, PN1000475; Chromium Next GEM Chip Q Single Cell Kit, PN-1000418; Dual Index Kit TS Set A, PN-1000251, 10x Genomics) and the User guide CG000527, Rev E.

For storage, the pre or post-hybridized nuclei suspensions were supplemented with 10% Glycerol and 0.1X Enhancer (PN-2000482, 10x Genomics), rested On ice for 00:10:00 , and then cryopreserved at 80 °C . For the hybridization step, between 500,000 and 1.5 million nuclei were used; hybridization was conducted for 20:00:00 at 42 °C across all samples. BC1-4 were used in all samples used in this study. Post-hybridization washes were carried out in a pool-wash setting as per User guide, with an additional wash included. Approximately 10,000 nuclei/sample were aimed for capture. An extra cycle was introduced for the Pre-Amplification PCR step. Indexing PCR conditions were tailored to attain the target library concentration of 50 nanomolar (nM) - 100 nanomolar (nM) . Libraries were QCed and quantified using a Fragment Analyzer 5200 (Agilent) and a Qbit Flex Fluorometer (ThemoFisher Scientific), respectively. The indexed libraries were sequenced using the Illumina NovaSeq 6000 system. The read configuration was Read 1: 28, i7: 10, i5: 10, and Read 2: 90. Libraries were sequenced to aim 20,000-30,000 read-pairs/nuclei.
The scrolls sections were washed in 1 mL of xylene for 00:10:00 each to dewax (1/3).
The scrolls sections were washed in 1 mL of xylene for 00:10:00 each to dewax (2/3).
The scrolls sections were washed in 1 mL of xylene for 00:10:00 each to dewax (3/3).
Followed by rehydration through 70% ethanol for 00:01:00 .
Followed by rehydration through 100% ethanol for 00:01:00 (2/2).
Followed by rehydration through 100% ethanol for 00:01:00 (1/2).
Followed by rehydration through 30% ethanol for 00:01:00 .
Followed by rehydration through 50% ethanol for 00:01:00 .

The pellet, consisting of released nuclei and undigested tissue, was resuspended in 250 µL of Ez Lysis buffer with 2% BSA (130-091-376, MACS BSA Stock Solution, Miltenyi) and 1 U/µL RNAse Inhibitor, followed by homogenization using a 1.5 mL pellet pestle.
This was supplemented with an additional 750 µL of the same lysis mix, with further disaggregation achieved through pipetting.
